question: Explain the three main types of monitoring in IT capacity planning.
expectedPoints: System Monitoring - tracks CPU, memory, disk, OS metrics
Network Monitoring - tracks bandwidth, latency, packet loss, network errors
Application Monitoring - tracks response time, transaction rates, error rates, user experience

question: What are the five golden signals in monitoring? Explain each briefly.
expectedPoints: Latency - time taken to process requests
Traffic - amount of demand/data flow through system
Errors - rate of failed requests/transactions
Saturation - degree to which resources are fully utilized
Availability - percentage of time system is operational

question: Differentiate between active monitoring and passive monitoring.
expectedPoints: Active monitoring injects test transactions/synthetic probes into system
Passive monitoring captures real traffic without injection
Active provides proactive testing, passive provides actual user experience data
Active useful for testing new deployments, passive for real-world insights

question: Define baseline monitoring and explain its importance in capacity planning.
expectedPoints: Baseline monitoring establishes normal performance patterns under typical conditions
Serves as reference for detecting anomalies and deviations
Helps distinguish between normal variation and actual problems
Critical for accurate capacity forecasting and trend analysis

question: Explain the relationship between monitoring metrics and capacity planning decisions.
expectedPoints: Monitoring provides data on current resource utilization
Trends help predict future capacity needs
Thresholds trigger alerts before critical failures occur
Historical data enables accurate forecasting and budget planning

question: What is the difference between SLI, SLO, and SLA?
expectedPoints: SLI (Service Level Indicator) - quantitative measure of service quality
SLO (Service Level Objective) - target value for SLI
SLA (Service Level Agreement) - contractual commitment with customers
Example: SLI=99.9% latency, SLO=maintain 99.9%, SLA=guarantee 99.9%

question: List and explain four key KPIs used in IT monitoring for capacity planning.
expectedPoints: CPU Utilization - percentage of processing capacity in use
Memory Utilization - RAM usage percentage
Response Time - time to handle user requests
Uptime - percentage of time system is available
Error Rate - percentage of failed transactions
